MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This question paper contains three sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each section is compulsory.
  3. Section A has 6 short answer type I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section B has 4 short answer type II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question no. 14 is a case-study based question with 2 sub-parts of 2 marks each.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

A bag contains cards numbered 1 to 25. Two cards are drawn at random, one after the other, without replacement. Find the probability that the number on each card is a multiple of 7.

 2
2.

If {tex}\vec{\text{a}}, \vec{\text{b}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\text{c}}{/tex} are unit vectors such that {tex}\vec{\text{a}}+\vec{\text{b}}+\vec{\text{c}}=\vec{0}{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}+\vec{\text{b}} \cdot \vec{\text{c}}+\vec{\text{c}} \cdot \vec{\text{a}}{/tex}.

 2
3.(i)

Find the general solution of the differential equation (x cos y)dy = ex(x log x + 1)dx.

 2

OR
(ii)

Find the value of (2a - 3b), if a and b represent respectively the order and the degree of the differential equation {tex}x\left[y\left(\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}\right)^3+x\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^2-\frac{y}{x} \frac{d y}{d x}\right]=0{/tex}.

 2
4.

Evaluate: {tex}\int_0^5 x \cdot \sqrt{5-x} d x{/tex}

 2
5.

If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} are three vectors, then find a vector perpendicular to both the vectors {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} and {tex}(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex}.

 2
6.

One bag contains 4 white and 5 black balls. Another bag contains 6 white and 7 black balls. A ball is transferred from first bag to the second bag and then a ball is drawn from the second bag. Find the probability that the ball drawn is white.

 2

SECTION B

Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.(i)

Find the distance between the following parallel lines:
{tex}\vec{r}=(2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k})+\lambda(\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}\vec{r}=(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})+\mu(\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Find the coordinates of the point where the line through (-1, 1, -8) and (5, -2, 10) crosses the ZX-plane.

 3
8.

Find the coordinates of the foot of the perpendicular drawn from the point (1, 3, 4) to the plane {tex}\vec r \cdot (2\hat i - \hat j + \hat k){/tex} + 3 = 0.

 3
9.

Find: {tex}\int \sin^{-1} \text {x dx}{/tex}

 3
10.(i)

Find the area of the region {(x, y) : x2 + y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 9, x + y {tex}\geq{/tex} 3}, using integration.

 3

OR
(ii)

Using integration, find the area of the region bounded by the parabola y2 = 4x, the lines x = 0 and x = 3 and the x-axis.

 3

SECTION C

Question numbers 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation (1 + sin x) {tex}\frac {dy}{dx}{/tex} = -x - y cos x, given y(0) = 1.

 4
12.

Find the vector equation of the plane passing through the intersection of the planes {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k})=7{/tex} and {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(2 \hat{i}+5 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k})=9{/tex} and passing through the point (2, 1, 3)

 4
13.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \cos x \cdot \tan ^{-1}{/tex} (sin x) dx

 4

OR
(ii)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{e^x}{\left(e^x+1\right)\left(e^x+3\right)} d x{/tex}

 4

Case-Study Based Question
14.

A biased die is tossed and respective probabilities for various faces to turn up are the following:

Face 1 2 3 4 5 6
Probability 0·1 0·24 0·19 0·18 0·15 K

Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

What is the value of K?

 2
(ii)

If a face showing an even number has turned up, then what is the probability that it is the face with 2 or 4?

 2

Code: 65/6/2 (set-2)

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This question paper contains three sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each section is compulsory.
  3. Section A has 6 short answer type I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section B has 4 short answer type II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question no. 14 is a case-study based question with 2 sub-parts of 2 marks each.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

A bag contains cards numbered 1 to 25. Two cards are drawn at random, one after the other, without replacement. Find the probability that the number on each card is a multiple of 7.

Solution:

The cards which are multiple of 7 are 7, 14,
{tex}\because{/tex} n(S) = 25C2, n(A) = 3C1 {tex}\times{/tex} 2C1
Required probability = {tex}\frac{{}^3C_1\times{}^2C_1}{{}^{25}C_2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3!\times2!\times2!\times23!}{2!\times25!}{/tex}‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
= {tex}\frac{3\times2\times2}{25\times24}=\frac1{50}{/tex}‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The probability of two cards which are multiple of 7 each is {tex}\frac1{50}{/tex}

 2
2.

If {tex}\vec{\text{a}}, \vec{\text{b}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\text{c}}{/tex} are unit vectors such that {tex}\vec{\text{a}}+\vec{\text{b}}+\vec{\text{c}}=\vec{0}{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}+\vec{\text{b}} \cdot \vec{\text{c}}+\vec{\text{c}} \cdot \vec{\text{a}}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} a, b and c are unit vectors,
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|\overrightarrow c\right|=1{/tex}
Now, {tex}\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow c=0(given){/tex}
{tex}\left(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow c\right)^2=0{/tex}
{tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2{/tex} {tex}+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} = 0
‚Äč‚Äč1 + 1 + 1 + 2{tex}(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} = 0
‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč{tex}(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} {tex}=\frac{-3}2{/tex}‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč

 2
3.(i)

Find the general solution of the differential equation (x cos y)dy = ex(x log x + 1)dx.

Solution:

The given differential equation may be written as
cos y dy ={tex}\left(e^{x} \log x+\frac{e^{x}}{x}\right) d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \cos y \, d y=\int(\log x) e^{x} d x+\int \frac{e^{x}}{x} d x+C{/tex}
where C is an arbitrary constant
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \sin y=(\log x) e^{x}-{/tex}{tex}\int \frac{1}{x} \cdot e^{x} d x+\int \frac{e^{x}}{x} d x+C{/tex} [integrating by parts]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \sin y=(\log x) e^{x}+C{/tex} this is the solution.

 2

OR
(ii)

Find the value of (2a - 3b), if a and b represent respectively the order and the degree of the differential equation {tex}x\left[y\left(\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}\right)^3+x\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^2-\frac{y}{x} \frac{d y}{d x}\right]=0{/tex}.

Solution:

Order (a) of differential equation = 2
Degree (b) of differential equation = 3
So, (2a - 3b) = 2 × 2 - 3 × 3 = 4 - 9 = -5

 2
4.

Evaluate: {tex}\int_0^5 x \cdot \sqrt{5-x} d x{/tex}

Solution:

Let {tex}I=\int_0^5x\sqrt{5-x}\;dx{/tex}
Using property {tex}\int_0^af(x)\;dx=\int_0^af\left(a-x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_0^5\left(5-x\right)\sqrt xdx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_0^55\sqrt xdx-\int_0^5\left(x\right)^\frac32dx{/tex}
{tex}I=5\left[\frac{x^\frac32}{\frac32}\right]_0^5-\left[\frac{x^\frac52}{\frac52}\right]_0^5{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac{5\times2}3\left[5^{\textstyle\frac32}\right]-\frac25\left(5^{\textstyle\frac52}\right){/tex}
{tex}I=\frac{250\sqrt5-150\sqrt5}{15}=\frac{20\sqrt5}3{/tex}

 2
5.

If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} are three vectors, then find a vector perpendicular to both the vectors {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} and {tex}(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex}.

Solution:

Given {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex},
{tex}\vec{c}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}
Now,
{tex}(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b){/tex} {tex}=\widehat i+\widehat j-2\widehat k-\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k=3\widehat j{/tex}
‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč{tex}(\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c)=-\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k+\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k=3\widehat k{/tex} ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äčwe know that , cross product of two vectors give the perpendicular vector to it.
So, {tex}(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b)\times(\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c){/tex} {tex}=\begin{vmatrix}\widehat i&\widehat j&\widehat k\\0&3&0\\0&0&3\end{vmatrix}{/tex}
‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč{tex}=\left(9-0\right)\widehat i-\left(0-0\right)\widehat j+\left(0-0\right)\widehat k{/tex} {tex}=9\widehat i{/tex}
‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚ÄčTherefore, the required perpendicular vector is {tex}9\widehat i{/tex}‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč.

 2
6.

One bag contains 4 white and 5 black balls. Another bag contains 6 white and 7 black balls. A ball is transferred from first bag to the second bag and then a ball is drawn from the second bag. Find the probability that the ball drawn is white.

Solution:

A white ball can be drawn in two mutually exclusive ways:

  1. By transferring a black ball from first to second bag, then drawing a white ball
  2. By transferring a white ball from first to second bag, then drawing a white ball

Consider the following events:
E1 = A black ball is transferred from first to second bag
E2 = A white ball is transferred from first to second bag
A = A white ball is drawn
Therefore, we have,
P(E1) = {tex}\frac{5}{9}{/tex}
P(E2) = {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex}
Now,
{tex}P\left(\frac {A}{ E_{1}}\right)=\frac{6}{14}{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac {A}{E_{2}}\right)=\frac{7}{14}{/tex}
Using the law of total probability, we get
Required probability = P(A) = P(E1)P({tex}\frac {A}{E_1}{/tex}) + P(E2)P({tex}\frac {A}{E_2}{/tex})
{tex}=\frac{5}{9} \times \frac{6}{14}+\frac{4}{9} \times \frac{7}{14}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{30}{126}+\frac{28}{126}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{58}{126}=\frac{29}{63}{/tex}

 2

SECTION B

Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.(i)

Find the distance between the following parallel lines:
{tex}\vec{r}=(2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k})+\lambda(\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}\vec{r}=(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})+\mu(\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec{r}=(2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k})+\lambda(\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex}
and {tex}\vec{r}=(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})+\mu(\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}\vec{a_1}=(2\widehat i+\widehat j-\widehat k){/tex} , {tex}\vec{a_2}=(\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k){/tex}
{tex}\vec b=\widehat i+\widehat j-\widehat k{/tex}
Distance between two parallel lines
{tex}d=\left|\frac{\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow{b_1}\right|}\right|{/tex} ...(i)
Now, {tex}\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}=-\widehat i-3\widehat j+2\widehat k{/tex}, {tex}\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=\sqrt{1+1+1}=\sqrt3{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow b\times\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right)=\left(\widehat i+\widehat j-\widehat k\right)\left(-\widehat i-3\widehat j+2\widehat k\right){/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow b\times\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right)=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&1&-1\\-1&-3&2\end{vmatrix}=-\widehat i-\widehat j-2\widehat k{/tex} putting in eqn (i)
{tex}d=\left|\frac{-\widehat i-\widehat j-2\widehat k}{\sqrt3}\right|{/tex}
{tex}d=\frac{\widehat i+\widehat j+2\widehat k}{\sqrt3}{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Find the coordinates of the point where the line through (-1, 1, -8) and (5, -2, 10) crosses the ZX-plane.

Solution:

Equation of line passing through point (-1, 1, -8) and (5, -2, 10)
{tex}\frac{x+1}{5+1}=\frac{y-1}{-2-1}=\frac{z+8}{10+8}=\lambda{/tex}
{tex}x=6\lambda-1, y=-3\lambda+1,{/tex} {tex}z=18\lambda-8{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\because{/tex} point crosses ZX plane so
y = 0
{tex}-3\lambda+1=0{/tex}
{tex}\lambda=\frac13{/tex}
So, putting in eq. (i)
x = 1, y = 0 and z = -2

 3
8.

Find the coordinates of the foot of the perpendicular drawn from the point (1, 3, 4) to the plane {tex}\vec r \cdot (2\hat i - \hat j + \hat k){/tex} + 3 = 0.

Solution:

Let, P (1, 3, 4) and {tex}\overrightarrow r.\left(2\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k\right)+3=0{/tex}
Cartesian equation of given plane = 2x - y + z + 3 = 0
Direction cosines of point = x1 - 1, y1 - 3, z1 - 4
Direction cosines of {tex}\overrightarrow n{/tex} = (2, -1, 1)
Now, {tex}\frac{x_1-1}2=\frac{y_1-3}{-1}=\frac{z_1-4}1=\lambda{/tex}
{tex}x_1=2\lambda+1,\;y_1=-\lambda+3,\;z_1=\lambda+4{/tex}
These points lies on the plane
So putting these value in equation of plane
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}4\lambda+2+\lambda-3+\lambda+4+3=0{/tex}
{tex}6\lambda=-6{/tex}
{tex}\lambda=-1{/tex}
So , x1 = -1, y1 = 4, z1 = 3
Therefore coordinates of foot of perpendicular are (-1, 4, 3)

 3
9.

Find: {tex}\int \sin^{-1} \text {x dx}{/tex}

Solution:

I = {tex}=\int \underbrace{\sin ^{-1} x}_I \cdot \underbrace{1}_{{II}} d x{/tex}
using integration by parts
I = {tex}\sin ^{-1} x \int 1 \cdot d x{/tex} - {tex}\int\left[\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right) \cdot \int 1 \cdot d x\right] d x{/tex}
= {tex}\sin ^{-1} x \cdot x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} \cdot x d x{/tex}
= {tex}x \cdot \sin ^{-1} x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} d x{/tex}
= {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} [put 1 - x2 = t, -2xdx = dt, xdx = - {tex}\frac{dt}{2}{/tex}]
= {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \int t^{\frac{-1}{2}} d t{/tex}
= {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right]+c{/tex} {using {tex}\int x^n d x=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}{/tex}}
= {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\sqrt{t}+c{/tex}
= {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\sqrt{1-x^2}+c{/tex}

 3
10.(i)

Find the area of the region {(x, y) : x2 + y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 9, x + y {tex}\geq{/tex} 3}, using integration.

Solution:


For intersection point
x2 + y2 = 9 and x + y = 3
We get x = 0 y = 0, x = 3 y = 0
Shaded area = area under circle - area under line
Area ={tex}\int\limits_0^3y_{circle}dx-\int_\limits0^3y_{line}dx{/tex}
{tex}=\int_\limits0^3\left(\sqrt{9-x^2}\right)dx-\int\limits_0^3\left(3-x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\frac x2\sqrt{9-x^2}+\frac92\sin^{-1}\left(\frac x3\right)\right]_0^3-\left[3x-\frac{x^2}2\right]_0^3{/tex}
{tex}=\left[0+\frac92\sin^{-1}\left(\frac33\right)-0-0\right]-\left[9-\frac92\right]{/tex}
Area = {tex}\frac92\times\frac\pi2-\frac92=\frac92\left(\frac\pi2-\frac12\right){/tex} sq.unit

 3

OR
(ii)

Using integration, find the area of the region bounded by the parabola y2 = 4x, the lines x = 0 and x = 3 and the x-axis.

Solution:


Here y2 = 4x is a right handed parabola having its vertex at the origin and x = 1, x = 4 are the lines parallel to the y-axis at x = 1 to x = 4 units distance.

Using integration by parts
Let {tex}\frac{e^x}{\left(e^x+1\right)\left(e^x+3\right)}=\frac A{\left(e^x+1\right)}+\frac B{\left(e^x+3\right)}{/tex}
ex = Aex + 3A + Bex + B
ex = (A + B)ex + 3A + B

{tex}A=-\frac12{/tex}, {tex}B=\frac32{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac12\int\frac{dx}{e^x+1}+\frac32\int\frac{dx}{e^x+3}{/tex}

Put 1 + e-x = u and 1 + 3e-x = v

Face 1 2 3 4 5 6
Probability 0·1 0·24 0·19 0·18 0·15 K

Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. What is the value of K? (2)
  2. If a face showing an even number has turned up, then what is the probability that it is the face with 2 or 4? (2)
Solution:

  1. P(1) + P(2) + P(3) + P(4) + P(5) + P(6)
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0.1 + 0.24 + 0.19 + 0.18 + 0.15 + K = 1
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} K = 0.14

  2. A: face shows 2 or 4
    B: even face have turned up
    Required probability = P (A/B) = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{A} \cap \mathrm{B})}{\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{B})}{/tex}
    here P (A{tex}\cap{/tex}B) = P(2) + P(4)
    = 0.24 + 0.18 = 0.42
    P(B) = P(2) + P(4) + P(6) = 0.56
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} P(A/B) = {tex}\frac{0.42}{0.56}{/tex} = {tex}\frac34{/tex}

A biased die is tossed and respective probabilities for various faces to turn up are the following: Face 1 2 3 4 5 6 Probability 0·1 0·24 0·19 0·18 0·15 K Based on the above information, answer the following questions: What is the value of K? (2) If a face showing an even number has turned up, then what is the probability that it is the face with 2 or 4? (2) Solution: P(1) + P(2) + P(3) + P(4) + P(5) + P(6) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0.1 + 0.24 + 0.19 + 0.18 + 0.15 + K = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} K = 0.14 A: face shows 2 or 4 B: even face have turned up Required probability = P (A/B) = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{A} \cap \mathrm{B})}{\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{B})}{/tex} here P (A {tex}\cap{/tex} B) = P(2) + P(4) = 0.24 + 0.18 = 0.42 P(B) = P(2) + P(4) + P(6) = 0.56 {tex}\therefore{/tex} P(A/B) = {tex}\frac{0.42}{0.56}{/tex} = {tex}\frac34{/tex} 4
